# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a Google Cloud Deployment Manager template for a Cloud Storage bucket to host static website assets. Grant allUsers read access so the website can serve files publicly. Set storageClass to STANDARD for performance. This is for our public-facing website storage on GCP.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

resources:
- name: {app_name}-bucket
  type: google.storage.v1.Bucket
  properties:
    bucketId:{app_name}
    storageClass:"STANDARD" # default is STANDARD, STANDARD_IA is for cold data like backups 
    uniformRouting:"false"  # this parameter indicates whether Google Cloud Storage should enforce a strict routing strategy across buckets (false by default)
    lifecycleRules:[
      {
        action:{
          type:"Delete",  # if no action field then Google Cloud Storage sets the bucket lifecycle to null.
          storageClass:"STANDARD_IA",  # "NEARLINE" and "STANDARD_IA" storage class are recommended as per cloud.google.com/storage/pricing#bucket_tier_standard_and_nearline_buckets. But "MULTI_REGIONAL" storage is not allowed.
          condition:{
            age:500,            # number of days since the creation of a new object before Google Cloud Storage should delete it
            withState:"active", 
            matchesObjectAcl:false  # true to enforce that all objects in this bucket have Object ACLs and Lifecycle rules that specify an action that does not affect STANDARD_IA storage class. The default is false, which means that you must either provide Object ACLs or Lifecycle rules (or both) that do not specify a delete action for objects in the bucket when a new object is created.
          }
        },
      }
    ]
  metadata:{
    accessControl:{
      listBucket: {
        entity:"allUsers", # "allAuthenticatedUsers", "projectEditor", "projectOwner", "serviceAccount"
        role:"READER", 
      }, 
    },  
    lifecycle: {
      bucketPolicyOnly:{ 
        enabled :true, 
      }, 
    }  
  }, 
}
